sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 21.60 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.21)

statement.latex: Let$G$be a finite group,${ Z}_{(p)}$the localization at$p$, and$_p$the field of$p$elements. Let$X$be the class of$_pG$-modules obtained by reduction of simple$G$-modules. Is it true that$_{(p)}G$is semiperfect if and only if each projective indecomposable$_pG$-module can be written as an$$-linear combination of modules in$X$inside the Grothendieck group of$_pG$? The ``only if'' direction is proved...

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory VI_BRIDGING; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
